hypothesis: Mutations at positions 5 and 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions by introducing aromatic residues at position 5 (to complement SSTR2 ECL2 residues 192/193/195/197) and hydrophobic residues at position 11 (to avoid steric clashes with SSTR3/5 TM6 residues 272/273/276/279). This should increase the selectivity margin by differentiating SSTR2 from off-targets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy must incorporate explicit selectivity optimization, particularly by targeting non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target interactions.

proposed_changes.rationale: Disrupt conserved-pocket contacts at non-pharmacophore positions to reduce off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the pharmacophore FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
Prioritize selectivity over binding strength by incorporating Δmargin into the scoring function, ensuring candidates are evaluated for their ability to outperform native SST-14 in selectivity.
